perturbation.original_text: Subject to termination pursuant to this Agreement, the Non Exclusive license granted by Licensor to Licensee shall be for an initial period of 36 months, commencing from the acceptance date, (the "Initial Period"). The Agreement may be renewed for an additional period of seven (7) years at the option of the Licensor. Upon any breach by Licensee of any representation, warranty, covenant and/or obligation hereunder, the Licensor may immediately terminate this Agreement and prohibit the Licensee's use of any and/or all of the Licensed Technology. In the event of such immediate termination Licensor shall retain all of it rights under this Agreement and applicable law including but not limited to the right to receive payments for all trades performed using the Licensed Technology.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to termination pursuant to this Agreement, the Non Exclusive license granted by Licensor to Licensee shall be for an initial period of 36 months, commencing from the acceptance date, (the "Initial Period"). The Agreement may be extended for an additional period of seven (7) years at the discretion of the Licensor. Upon any breach by Licensee of any representation, warranty, covenant and/or obligation hereunder, the Licensor may immediately terminate this Agreement and prohibit the Licensee's use of any and/or all of the Licensed Technology. In the event of such immediate termination Licensor shall retain all of it rights under this Agreement and applicable law including but not limited to the right to receive payments for all trades performed using the Licensed Technology.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'renewed at the option of' to 'extended at the discretion of' introduces ambiguity. 'Option' implies a formal process or right to renew, potentially requiring a specific action or notification. 'Discretion' suggests a more informal, unilateral decision-making process by the Licensor. This could lead to disputes about whether a renewal requires a formal option exercise or if the Licensor simply decides on its own. This is contradictory as it will be hard to determine if there is a written notification or not, since the definition of 'option' implies a formal process in most agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Initial Period shall be extended by mutual written of the parties within 45 days of the close of the Initial Period.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement may be 'renewed for an additional period of seven (7) years at the option of the Licensor.' The changed text uses 'extended at the discretion of the Licensor.' This change from 'option' to 'discretion' introduces a significant ambiguity regarding the process for extending the agreement. The contradicted text requires a 'mutual written agreement of the parties within 45 days of the close of the Initial Period' to extend the Initial Period. The original text with 'option' could be interpreted as requiring a formal process that aligns with the mutual written agreement. However, 'discretion' suggests a unilateral decision by the Licensor, creating a direct contradiction with the requirement for mutual written agreement. This ambiguity leads to a strong in-text contradiction because it is unclear whether the extension requires a formal written agreement or is solely at the discretion of the Licensor.

perturbation.original_text: The Licensor shall provide Technical Support Services relating to the maintenance and support of the Software and the Licensee shall pay the Licensor $2,500 per month for such Technical Support Services.
perturbation.changed_text: The Licensor may provide Technical Support Services relating to the maintenance and support of the Software and the Licensee shall pay the Licensor $2,500 per month for such Technical Support Services.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall provide' to 'may provide' fundamentally alters the obligation of the Licensor regarding technical support. 'Shall' implies a mandatory duty, while 'may' indicates a discretionary offering. This change contradicts the later sections that discuss the *manner* of providing technical support, creating uncertainty about whether the Licensor is actually obligated to provide any support at all, which makes the agreement contradictory. Especially since the Licensee is paying for the Technical Support Services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (e) Licensee and Licensor Responsibilities i. The Licensor shall provide customer support to Licensee on terms and conditions set forth herein below.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'The Licensor shall provide Technical Support Services,' which is a mandatory obligation. The changed text modifies this to 'The Licensor may provide Technical Support Services,' making it discretionary. This contradicts the 'Licensee and Licensor Responsibilities' clause which states 'The Licensor shall provide customer support to Licensee,' because the Licensor is now paying for a service that the Licensor may or may not provide. The change introduces ambiguity regarding whether the support will actually be provided and thus creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All notices required or permitted under this Agreement will be in writing and delivered by confirmed facsimile transmission, by courier or overnight delivery service, or by certified mail, and in each instance will be deemed given upon receipt. All communications to a party will be sent to the address of the party set forth in the preamble above or to such other address as may be specified by such party to the other in accordance with the Section called Contact Information. Either party may change its address for notices under this Agreement by giving written notice to the other party by the means specified in this Section.
perturbation.changed_text: All notices required or permitted under this Agreement will be in writing and delivered by confirmed facsimile transmission, by email, by courier or overnight delivery service, or by certified mail, and in each instance will be deemed given upon receipt. All communications to a party will be sent to the address of the party set forth in the preamble above or to such other address as may be specified by such party to the other in accordance with the Section called Contact Information. Either party may change its address for notices under this Agreement by giving written notice to the other party by the usual methods specified in this Section.
perturbation.explanation: By inserting email, we are now contradicting the last sentance where it is being stated to communicate by the usual methods. The use of 'usual methods' becomes vague since the agreement doesn't clarify if email is considered a 'usual' method or if it needs to be specified somewhere else in the agreement, leading to ambiguity and potential disputes over the validity of notices sent via email.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE VIII CONTACT INFORMATION Licensor Information: iii. If required by the Licensee, the Licensor product managers and engineers will host free Web-based training seminars covering a variety of topics to facilitate the use of the Software. These seminars will be broadcast on a periodic basis, provided, however, that the Licensor is under no obligation to provide them on any specific schedule. iv. Any software, modules, or other methods of communication developed by the Licensor in performing duties under this Agreement shall be kept secure from access by the unauthorized third parties. Any failure of such security shall be deemed, notwithstanding anything to the contrary, a failure of the Primary Function of the Software. Account Manager: Michael C. Caska Telephone: 917-226-9630 Fax: 646-607-9711 E-mail: mcaska@caskaco.com Billing: Michael C. Caska Telephone: 917-226-9630 Fax: 646-607-9711 E-mail: mcaska@caskaco.com Tech Support: Michael C. Caska Telephone: 917-226-9630 Fax: 646-607-9711 E-mail: mcaska@caskaco.com Written Notices: SFG Financial Corporation 575 Madison Avenue, 8th Floor, New York, NY 10022 Att: Michael C. Caska Licensee Information: Account Manager: Name: Fred Miller Telephone 212-605-0200 Fax: 212-605-0222 E-mail:_____________________________ Billing: Name: Fred Miller Telephone 212-605-0200 Fax: 212-605-0222 E-mail:_____________________________ Written Notices: 551 FX IB Associates LLC 575 Madison Avenue, 8th Floor, New York, NY 10022 Att: Fred Miller
perturbation.justification: The original text specified methods of notice delivery and allowed for changing the address via the 'means specified in this Section'. The changed text introduces 'email' and vaguely refers to 'usual methods specified in this Section'. This directly contradicts Article VIII (CONTACT INFORMATION), which lists email addresses for specific contacts but does *not* explicitly designate email as a valid method for 'Written Notices' to SFG Financial Corporation and 551 FX IB Associates LLC. The contradiction arises because adding email as a general method, while the contact information only specifies email for certain roles, creates ambiguity. The 'usual methods' now conflicts with the specific and limited context of email use within the Contact Information section.
